{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [vs-cron](https://mkloubert.github.io/vs-cron/)\n2. [Cron Tasks - Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=zokugun.cron-tasks)\n3. [zokugun/vscode-cron](https://github.com/zokugun/vscode-cron)\n4. [Cron Jobs - Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=mkloubert.vs-cron)\n5. [AaronFriel/vscode-cron](https://github.com/aaronfriel/vscode-cron)\n\n# VS Code Cron Tasks Extension - How to Use\n\nThere are multiple cron extensions available. The most actively maintained is **Cron Tasks** by zokugun.[2]\n\n## Installation\n\nInstall via VS Code Quick Open (Ctrl+P) or search the marketplace for \"cron-tasks\".[2]\n\n## Basic Setup\n\nAdd configuration to your &#96;settings.json&#96;:[2][3]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n\"cronTasks.tasks\": [\n    {\n        \"at\": \"* * * * *\",\n        \"run\": \"cronTasks.showTestMessage\"\n    }\n]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- &#96;at&#96;: Define the schedule using a cron expression[2]\n- &#96;run&#96;: Specify the VS Code command to execute[2]\n\n## Key Features\n\n**Testing**: Use the built-in test command &#96;cronTasks.showTestMessage&#96; to display a notification.[2]\n\n**Debugging**: View debug information in the Output panel under the \"Cron Tasks\" channel (View → Output). Enable &#96;\"cronTasks.debug\": true&#96; to auto-focus the debug output.[2]\n\n**Debug Mode**: Use &#96;\"cronTasks.debug\": \"useBlank\"&#96; to test without executing the actual task.[2]\n\n## Alternative Extension\n\nThe older **vs-cron** extension (by mkloubert) is now deprecated[4] but offers more advanced features like executing scripts and commands with additional configuration options like timezone, execution conditions, and parallel execution settings.[1]",
      "title": "Web search results for query: VS Code Cron Tasks extension how to use"
    }

}
